resources.string: You\'ve reached the edge of the realm.

Grant calendar access and Candela narrates your agenda — today, tomorrow, and the week ahead. Your events are read on your device only and never leave the phone. You can revoke access anytime in Android Settings.
Will keep its place in the list
Pins to the front of the carousel
Search AO3 by tag, fandom, or character
Search Standard Ebooks\' hand-curated public-domain classics
Search Radio Browser — community, public, and college stations worldwide
Tune in to KVMR — live community radio from Nevada City
Search messages in your selected Discord server
No dictionary or browser app found.
The voice engine is taking longer than expected. Try again, or back out to the chapter list.
Cold-start of a slow voice or large chapter can take a moment.
Beautiful work. The story is over — for now. Where would you like to go next?
Now playing: %1$s. %2$s. Tap to open reader.
Search the text of every downloaded chapter.
Pick up an audiobook to listen and Candela remembers your place.
Asking the librarian about the last few chapters.
Here\'s what happened in the last few chapters.
Quick chapter summaries from your AI provider.
Save speed for this book only
Use this speed for all books
Smoother pitch-shifted audio. Applies on next chapter.
Faster pitch shifting. Grittier at non-neutral pitch.
Open per-voice lexicon and Kokoro language
Open Voice Library for per-voice pronunciation rules and phonemizer overrides.
The voice or chapter text is taking longer than expected. Try again, or pick a different voice — some take a moment to warm up.
Drop a marker at the current position
Resume from the marker you set
Ask the librarian to summarize the last few chapters
Chat about plot, characters, pacing, and craft
Try a different search term or clear the search to see your full library.
Your cloud data will remain intact. You can sign back in to resume syncing.

This permanently deletes all your synced data from the cloud — your library, reading positions, bookmarks, voice settings, and follows. Data on this device is not affected, and you stay signed in.\n\nThis cannot be undone.
Your cloud data has been deleted.
Couldn\'t delete your cloud data. Please try again.

Removes the PCM cache for every chapter of this fiction across every voice variant. Replays will re-render once. The fiction stays in your library; your read progress and library state are not affected.
Your read progress will be lost. You can re-add it from Browse anytime, but the position you\'ve reached won\'t be restored.
Downloading all chapters — will wait for Wi-Fi
No highlights yet — the file is a placeholder.
Pick a provider in Settings → AI to start chatting.
Ask anything about \"%1$s\" — plot, characters, pacing, craft. The librarian won\'t spoil what\'s ahead.
Ask anything about this fiction. The librarian won\'t spoil what\'s ahead.
Image input not supported on this provider — ignoring attached image, sending text only.
No wings reachable. Confirm your palace daemon is configured under Settings → Memory Palace.
Subscribe to any feed that publishes RSS or Atom. Candela treats each feed as one fiction; each item is a chapter.
Paste a feed URL above to subscribe, or pick from the suggested list below.
Tap to browse curated feeds (Buddhist & dharma, more coming).
Audio podcast — Candela narrates show notes only
Tap to subscribe to a Craigslist regional feed by region + category.
Pick a region and a category to preview the feed URL.
Subscribed: %1$s. See it under Browse → RSS.
Candela reads books, guides, and articles to you with calming voices. Free, forever, no ads.
What would you like to hear?
Pick a starting point. You can always add more later.
Plain-English guides about computers, the internet, and getting set up.
Add a book from a website
We noticed a link on your clipboard — tap to use it.
Paste a link to a story, article, or book.
Each voice is a small free download. You can change it any time.
Install Google TTS for built-in voices, or download a Piper voice (~14 MB) below.
What do you want to read?
You can always change these later in Settings.
Warm American narrator — clear and steady.
British, gentle and even — good for long sessions.
Friendly American narrator — easy to follow.
British, soft-spoken — quiet hours and bedtime.
No settings match “%1$s”. Try a different term or clear the search.
Voice, speed, sleep timer, Do Not Disturb.
Browse and switch between available voices.
Review past chats and delete history.
Toggle backends — Fiction, Audio streams, Voice bundles.
Accessible DAISY library · partner API key.
Debug overlay, log ring, advanced toggles.
Save, edit, and organize teleprompter scripts.
Flip horizontally for beam-splitter teleprompter glass.
Record with the selfie camera (off = rear camera).
Every setting on one long page (legacy).
Azure HD & Dragon HD — bring your own key.
One episode from your sources — HN, arXiv, RSS, GitHub.
Default download mode, Wi-Fi, update interval, cache.
Allowed. Tap to manage in Android settings.
Blocked — tap to allow in Android settings.
On-device reminders before benefits deadlines you scan. Turn this off to silence them all — your saved deadlines aren\'t deleted and return when you turn it back on.
No configurable sources yet — enable a source under Plugins to set it up here.
Shake during the fade-out to add 15 more minutes.
Follow the narrated sentence down the page automatically.
Dim the off-focus lines and centre the active line for distraction-free reading.
Tint the reading page only — sepia, cream, or high-contrast. Doesn\'t change the rest of the app.
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
Applies to the chapter text only, not the rest of the app.
The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. Reading should feel comfortable.
How the reader marks the words being narrated.
Reset highlight color to theme accent
Bookshare requires a partner API key issued by Benetech. With a key stored, you can search and browse the accessible-book catalog. Downloading protected titles needs a separate partner agreement.
No partner key · Bookshare stays locked
How many items each Auto category exposes (Library / Follows / Recent / New). 6 matches Google\'s head-unit guideline; large libraries may prefer 8 or 12.
Flag inappropriate content from third-party sources
The Candela user guide — narrated, and it works offline.
Show the first-launch welcome again — no restart needed.
Candela is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3.0. You\'re free to use, study, share, and modify it under those terms. It is built on the open-source libraries listed below.
Review and manage past chats and chapter recaps.
Pick a voice and hear samples.
Smoother pitch-shifted audio. ~20%% extra CPU per chapter render.
Faster pitch shifting. Some grittiness at non-neutral pitch.
Foreign passages switch to a matching voice. Kokoro voices only.
Off — all text reads in your selected voice.
Teach the voice how to say specific names and words.
Seconds the +N / -N buttons jump per tap.
Skip-back: jump to start when within
Past this point in a chapter, the prev-track button rewinds to the start. Within it, jumps to the previous chapter. Off (0s) always goes to the previous chapter.
Minutes the timer extends when you shake during the 10-second fade tail.
Start timer in Bedtime or Do Not Disturb
When your phone enters Bedtime, Sleep, or Do Not Disturb mode, a sleep timer starts automatically.
Turn on Do Not Disturb during a timer
The reverse: while a sleep timer is running, silence notifications with Do Not Disturb, then restore your previous setting when it ends. Needs Do Not Disturb access.
How long a new sleep timer runs when you start one without picking a time.
Shake your device during the timer\'s fade-out to keep listening.
Download each chapter when you start playing it. Uses the least storage.
Download every chapter as soon as you add a book, so it\'s ready offline.
Download the current chapters, then auto-fetch new ones as they\'re published.
Defer downloads until you\'re on an unmetered network.
How often to check followed books for new chapters.
Pause briefly when the voice falls behind, then resume cleanly.
Drain through underruns; no buffering spinner.
Cache every chapter of every library fiction in the background. Aggressive disk + CPU use; gapless playback everywhere.
Cache the next few chapters only (1-3 on add, +2 on chapter end). Lighter on disk.
How much disk to use for cached audiobook PCM. Larger = more chapters playable instantly; smaller = less disk used.
Frees %1$s of disk. Chapters you replay will re-render the first time you play them. Library and reading positions are not affected.
How many chapters ahead the cache renders. Smaller = less disk + bandwidth; larger = more chapters ready for instant playback.
Network timeout budget for source plugins. Aggressive fails fast on flaky links; Patient gives slow APIs more room.
Wait for the voice to warm up before playback starts.
Start playback immediately; accept silence at chapter start.
Steady — identical text plays the same each time.
Use a higher-contrast variant of Library Nocturne. Auto-enables when TalkBack is active.
Disable non-essential animations (chip slides, brass shimmer, page-transition curves). Auto-enables when the system \"Remove animations\" setting is on.
Increase tap-target minimums from 48dp to 64dp where feasible. Auto-enables when Switch Access is active.
Extra pause between sentences when TalkBack is active.
Controls how TalkBack reads chapter headers.
Multiplier on top of Android\'s system font scale.
Escape hatch for RTL testing and locale-mismatch overrides.
These settings improve Candela for users who rely on TalkBack, Switch Access, or other accessibility tools. Most adapt automatically when assistive services are detected.
Open the accessibility documentation in your browser.
TalkBack is active. The screen-reader-related settings on this page are taking effect right now.

The screen-reader-related settings below activate when you turn on TalkBack in Android Settings → Accessibility. Some devices ship with TalkBack pre-installed; on others you\'ll need to install Android Accessibility Suite from the Play Store.
What to show when a fiction has no cover image.
Classic minimalist · author initial on dark
Bold magical · sun-disk + EB Garamond title
Real cover when available, dim sigil outline otherwise
Real cover images are always used when available. This setting controls what we render when a fiction has no cover, the cover URL has expired, or the load fails.
Master multiplier on every transition. Off makes animations instant — useful with assistive tech or on slower devices.
Brass-ember sparks + chapter-completion flare. None silences decorative particles; Lush doubles the count.
How loading placeholders look. Off = plain rectangle, Pulse = breathing rectangle, Sigil = rotating brass sigil.
How deep the brass breath cycles on loading dots, the player sigil, and skeleton tiles.
Encrypt API keys and tokens with a passphrase before syncing them to the cloud. The same passphrase is needed on every device.
Sign in to sync library, follows, positions, and secrets across devices.
Sign-in unlocks Premium chapters and your Follows list.
Enter a passphrase for secrets sync
How Candela sounds — voice, speed, cadence.
How chapter text and the reader behave.
Trade memory and CPU for smoother playback.
Smart features — Recap, character lookup, chat.
Where stories come from and how often we check for updates.
Fiction sources, audio streams, and voice bundles.
Narrate the full publisher article instead of just the headline digest. Off by default — uses Google\'s internal endpoint (may break on their changes) and is intended for personal feed-reader use.
New-chapter events from your Royal Road follows.
Live programs starting on KVMR Community Radio.
Updates to followed Wikipedia articles. (No diff poller yet — see #383 follow-up.)
Sign-in for fiction sources that need it.
Browse your local mempalace as fictions (LAN only).
Bring your own Azure key for HD Neural and Dragon HD voices.
A compact pipeline-state card floats above the reader.
Pipeline · engine · Azure · network · events · export.
Show the first-launch welcome flow again. Restart the app to see it.
Paste a fiction URL — we\'ll auto-detect the source.
Supported: Royal Road · AO3 · GitHub · Gutenberg · arXiv · RSS · Wikipedia · direct EPUB
Where do you want to add this from?
Several sources can handle this URL. Pick the one that fits best.
Sign in lifts the 60 req/hr anon cap to 5,000 req/hr and unlocks repository READMEs as fictions. read:user + public_repo only.
ON. Sign out and back in to upgrade to the `repo` scope (read/write to private + public repos).
OFF. Current token still carries the `repo` scope; sign out and back in to drop down to `public_repo`.
Sign-in requests `repo` (full repo, read/write, includes private).
Sign-out clears the local token; use this to revoke Candela\'s authorization on GitHub\'s side too.
Session expired — sign in again to recover.
Point your camera at a printed page, or pick a photo, and Candela reads it aloud. Scan several pages to make one document.
To scan printed text, Candela needs camera access. Nothing leaves your device — text recognition runs entirely offline.
Camera access is off. You can still scan by picking a photo from your gallery below, or turn the camera on in system Settings.
Capture this page for text recognition
Scan a printed page to read aloud
Finish a chapter and your reading stats will appear here.
Time figures are estimates based on the length of the chapters you\'ve finished — Candela doesn\'t track exact playback time yet.

Candela is run by TechEmpower, a nonprofit. Grants that keep the app free need impact numbers across everyone — never you. You can choose to share an anonymous, rounded monthly summary to help. Nothing is ever sent automatically.
Thanks — this month\'s summary has been shared.
This is exactly what will be shared — nothing more. You choose where it goes (email, a message, a form). Candela never sends it for you.
Coarse monthly totals, rounded — hours, chapters, and books
Which built-in sources you used — names only, never counts
The month and the app version (major.minor only)
Your name, email, or any account
Your device, location, or IP address
Titles, links, or anything you actually read
Anything that could point back to you
A report you share lives wherever you send it. Candela keeps no copy, and because it carries no identifier, TechEmpower can\'t trace one back to you.
How the optional anonymous summary works

Candela is run by TechEmpower, a nonprofit. The grants that keep the app free — and radically private — need aggregate impact numbers: total hours listened, chapters and books finished, and the range of sources people use, summed across everyone who chooses to share. Never you specifically.

There\'s no switch to leave on and nothing is ever sent automatically. When you want to help, open your Listening Stats screen, preview the exact anonymous summary, and share it — that one tap is the whole feature.
Narrating chapters offline. This keeps going in the background.
Paste or type your text, pick a voice, and Candela narrates it into a chaptered audiobook you can keep and share — all offline, no account.
Paste a chapter, an article, a story…
No pages yet. Tap “Scan pages” to capture a document, or add photos from your gallery.
Turn a stack of paper into one clean PDF you can email — no scanner needed. Pages are cleaned up and combined on your device.
Scanning and PDF creation happen on your device. Your documents never leave your phone.
The document scanner isn\'t available on this device. You can still add page photos from your gallery.
Answer a few questions to find local programs you may qualify for.
Works offline. Your answers stay on this device — nothing is sent anywhere.
These programs and questions are a preview. Final program rules come from TechEmpower.
Answer a few questions above, then tap “See my results.”
These details are not yet verified.
We could not open the program list. Please update the app and try again.
Answer a few quick questions to find local help you may be eligible for. Works offline.
Take a photo of a benefits letter, or type its form number, and we will explain what it means and what to do.
Works offline. The letter\'s text stays on this device.
These explainers are a preview. Final, verified explainers come from TechEmpower.
We don\'t have a verified explainer for this yet
We will not guess what your letter says. You can hear it read aloud, or call 211 for free help understanding it.
This explainer is not yet verified.
We could not open the explainer library. Please update the app and try again.
Photograph a confusing benefits notice — we will explain what it means, in English or Spanish. Works offline.
Some help is just one phone call away. Pick a program — we will give you the number, what to say, and a place to jot down the answers.
These call scripts are a preview. Verified numbers and details come from TechEmpower.
Notes are kept on this device.
Number not yet verified — connects you through 211.
We could not open the call cards. Please update the app and try again.
Program phone numbers, what to say, and a checklist — with help beating call anxiety. Works offline.
Link Google Drive and pick a folder — the documents inside become listenable here. We only see the folder you choose.
Google Drive isn\'t set up in this version of the app yet.
No documents yet. Scan an ID, pay stub, award letter, or benefit card to keep it safe and ready.
Your documents are encrypted on this device and never leave your phone. Keep proofs ready for benefits applications.
Your documents are locked. Unlock with your fingerprint, face, or screen lock to view them.
This device has no screen lock set. Your documents are still encrypted, but add a screen lock for an extra layer.
Save these pages to your wallet
What kind of document is this?
Heads up: some programs only accept income proof from the last %1$d days. Consider recapturing.
A %1$s can be used as proof for:
We don\'t have a verified list of programs for this document type yet.
Sample list — the verified program list is provided by TechEmpower.
Save the facts you type on every form once. Candela keeps them encrypted on your device and offers to fill matching fields when you scan a form.
Save your details once — Candela fills matching form fields for you.
This erases your saved details from this device. It can\'t be undone.
Your profile is saved and locked.
Confirm it\'s you to view or change your saved details.
Never save your Social Security Number here. When a form asks for it, type it directly — Candela won\'t store it or offer to fill it.
Social Security Number — type it yourself; never saved.
Fill this field with your saved value: %1$s
On a scanned form, matching fields show a chip like these — tap to fill.
Photograph a benefits letter and Candela will find the deadline, then remind you before it — all on your device.
We couldn\'t find a date on that letter. Try a clearer photo, or set a reminder from a common renewal below.
Candela uses the camera only while you scan a letter. The photo is read on your device and never uploaded.
Camera is off. You can still choose a photo of your letter from your gallery.
Capture a photo of the notice
Dates we found — pick the deadline
Confirm the exact date printed on your notice.
Shown on your lock screen. Avoid case numbers or dollar amounts.
For reminders at an exact time, allow exact alarms in settings. Reminders still work without it.
%1$s is due %2$s. Open Candela for details.
Photograph a letter — we\'ll remind you before the deadline.
Photograph a paper form, fill it in on your phone, and export a completed PDF you can email or print. No printer needed.
No fields yet. Choose a tool above, then tap the form where you want to write, check a box, or sign.
Form page. Use the field list below to add and edit entries.
Everything stays on your device — the form, what you type, and your signature never leave your phone.
Signature drawing area. Draw your signature with your finger.
Tap the form to place your selected tool. Every field also appears in the list below for editing.
